title: Alerts API — Create and manage alert rules via API
content:
Programmatically create, update, and delete alert rules.

**Base URL:** https://api.clouddash.io/v2/alerts

**Create an alert rule — POST /v2/alerts**
```json
{
  "name": "High CPU on production servers",
  "condition": {
    "metric": "CPUUtilization",
    "operator": "gt",
    "threshold": 85,
    "for_minutes": 5
  },
  "resource_filter": {
    "tags": {"Environment": "production"}
  },
  "severity": "high",
  "notify": [
    {"type": "slack", "channel_id": "C01234ABCDE"},
    {"type": "pagerduty", "integration_key": "abc123"}
  ],
  "cooldown_minutes": 30
}
```

**Response (201 Created):** Returns the full alert rule object with its id field.

**Manage rules:**
- GET /v2/alerts — list all rules (paginated, max 100 per page)
- GET /v2/alerts/{id} — get a specific rule
- PATCH /v2/alerts/{id} — update fields (partial update supported)
- DELETE /v2/alerts/{id} — delete a rule
- POST /v2/alerts/{id}/mute — mute for a duration ({"minutes": 60})

**Webhook notifications:**
Set notify type to 'webhook' with a url field. CloudDash will POST a JSON payload to your endpoint when the alert fires. Payloads are signed with HMAC-SHA256 using your webhook secret — verify the X-CloudDash-Signature header.
